prompt: Photorealistic turntable sprite sheet for a wine education website: twelve sequential views of the same Albarino grape cluster rotating around its vertical stem axis, arranged in exactly 3 columns by 4 rows. Each panel must contain one complete grape cluster with generous empty background margin on all four sides. The cluster must remain centered, similarly sized, and fully visible in every panel; no berry, stem, shadow, leaf, or object may touch the cell edge. Show small green-gold berries, thick skins, compact Atlantic cluster, translucent highlights, fresh pale bloom. Use consistent dark teal studio-vineyard background, consistent natural side lighting, realistic grape morphology, authentic wine-grape scale, subtle dusty bloom, and a museum-quality macro photography look. This must be useful for a drag-to-rotate object viewer, so each panel should represent a distinct rotation angle around the same bunch. No text, no labels, no watermark, no numbers, no logos, no hands, no bottle, no glass, no cartoon style.
use_case: photorealistic grape-cluster turntable sprite sheet for interactive wine education
style: macro studio photography, realistic wine grape morphology, consistent lighting, generous photo margins
composition: portrait 3 by 4 grid, twelve clean full-object turntable frames, each grape cluster centered with wide empty margin and no cropping
constraints: no text, no labels, no watermark, no panel numbers, no visible grid lines required, no clipped top, no clipped bottom, no repeated fragments, no people, no bottle, no glass, no illustration
